packages.summary: Automatic differentiation primitives: forward-mode dual numbers, a reverse-mode evaluation tape, and the Scalarish numeric trait they share.

Cross-domain arithmetic: the math/add, math/sub, math/mul, math/div, and reduction entry points that coerce mixed-domain operands through the promotion lattice and route symbolic inputs to the CAS when it is loaded.
The numbers/bigint domain: arbitrary-precision integer literals and values, their exact arithmetic, and promotion into rational.
The numbers/bool domain: boolean literals and values as the base of the number-promotion lattice, with edges widening into the integer and float domains.

The numbers/cas domain: the core computer-algebra layer. Defines the symbolic expression tree (CasExpr), its value citizen, the conversions to and from surface Expr/Value, and the cas/var and cas/simplify functions other CAS crates build on.
Symbolic differentiation and integration over the numbers/cas expression tree: the diff and integrate-sym functions plus an extensible registry of per-operator differentiation rules.
Evaluation of numbers/cas symbolic expressions: the eval-cas function that walks a CasExpr against an environment, in either numeric or symbolic mode, and the surface Expr/CasExpr bridge it uses.
The number-literal codec surface: helpers that build the numeric-plugin-v1 descriptor symbols and table values advertising a numeric codec or method provider to the runtime.
The numbers/complex domain: complex literals and values, their arithmetic, and the promotion edges from i64, f64, and rational into the complex sink of the scalar lattice.

Shared substrate for scalar number domains: the number-value shape and browse table (value_shape), the shared number-literal shape and class (literal), and the scalar-domain spec, literal matcher, and op-loop installer (scalar). The canonical number-domain symbol registry and promotion-lattice documentation live in domains.

Exotic number domains, currently the lazy continued-fraction domain (numbers/cf): infinite-precision reals carried as continued-fraction coefficient streams, their builtin constants, and the as-f64 truncation function.
Double-precision float number domain with arithmetic and complex promotion.
The fixed-width integer domains (numbers/i8 .. numbers/i128, numbers/u8 .. numbers/u128): their literals, values, and the widening promotion edges through the signed and unsigned integer lattice.
The numbers/f32 domain: single-precision floating-point literals and values, their scalar arithmetic, and promotion into f64.
Function number domain: callable function values built over CAS or native bodies, with fn, call, and grad operations for the Func domain.
The numbers/i64 domain: 64-bit signed-integer literals and values, their scalar arithmetic, and promotion edges into f64 and rational.
Numeric evaluation surface: the numeric domain exposes numeric-diff, integrate, ode-solve, and composed pipelines over a registry of pluggable differentiator, quadrature, and ODE-solver backends.

Umbrella library for the sim-numbers family: NumbersPreludeLib installs the standard number domains and tensor backends (arithmetic, f64/i64/bigint, rational, complex, CAS, func, tensor and its specializations, numeric, quad, and rk) into a runtime in one call.
Quadrature and finite-difference backends for the numeric domain: fixed and adaptive integration rules plus finite-difference differentiators, packaged as a registered numeric plugin library.
The numbers/rational domain: exact rational literals and values built over bigint numerator/denominator pairs, their reduced arithmetic, and promotion edges to and from the integer and f64 domains.
Runge-Kutta ODE integrators for the numeric domain: fixed-step and adaptive solver backends registered as numeric ode-solve plugins.
Probability, descriptive statistics, and fairness metric helpers for f64 number-domain data.

The n-dimensional tensor number domain: the uniform Tensor value, its domain registration and constructors (tensor, vec, mat, ...), and the SpecTensor interface that specialized element-type backends plug into. Tensor shape and dtype stay canonical while the open TensorStorage contract supplies boxed host or loadable resident storage. Observation through Tensor::cell, Tensor::cells, and Tensor::materialize is checked so encoding and projection report resident readback failures.
Tensor broadcasting specialization: element-wise binary and unary tensor operations with NumPy-style shape broadcasting and promotion rules.
Bit-tensor specialization: a packed-word boolean tensor element type and its SpecTensor backend, with bitwise operations over the tensor domain.
Complex-float tensor specialization: a (real, imag) f64-pair tensor element type and its SpecTensor backend for the complex tensor domain.
f64 tensor specialization: a contiguous f64 tensor element type and its SpecTensor backend for the f64 tensor domain.
i64 tensor specialization: a contiguous i64 tensor element type and its SpecTensor backend, with overflow-checked operations that fall back to the bigint domain.
Linear-algebra operations over the tensor domain: dot, matmul, cross, transpose, det, inv, trace, norm, and the eye/zeros/ones constructors, registered as a tensor library.
Rational-i64 tensor specialization: a normalized (numerator, denominator) i64-pair tensor element type and its SpecTensor backend for the rational tensor domain.
Repository automation wrapper for sim-numbers generated documentation and policy checks.
